title: Aureline provider-linked object header, notification / activity-center / support-export reuse, and degradation-event vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the provider_link_header_record, handoff_reason_reuse_record, and header_degradation_event_record frozen in docs/providers/provider_link_header_and_handoff_contract.md. The header makes a provider-linked object render as a typed companion to local truth: provider class, canonical host, object type, local-versus-provider source, last-sync time, freshness tier, scope summary, edit mode (draft / publish / browser-only / inspect-only / blocked-for-repair), and acting identity are visible together. The reuse record keeps provider class, scope, freshness, handoff reason, and acting identity intact when a provider-linked object is rendered outside the originating panel (notifications, activity center, support exports, audit events, companion / CLI / AI capture). The degradation event names how a header degrades for stale, revoked, offline, mirror-derived, or policy-blocked transitions and which inspect / copy / export actions remain. Raw URLs, raw tokens, raw provider payloads, raw callback bodies, and raw delegated-token bodies never cross this boundary; records carry opaque refs and structured fields only. The eventual provider-mode crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new provider_class, edit_mode_class, freshness_tier_class, local_or_provider_source_class, degradation_class, retained_action_class, removed_action_class, or reuse_surface_class is additive-minor and requires a provider_link_header_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

provider_link_header_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this provider-link-header vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / queue-review rollups / admin-reconciliation consoles. Raw URLs, raw tokens, raw callback bodies, raw delegated-token bodies, and raw provider payloads never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short human-readable sentence safe to show on desktop, CLI, notification, provider-linked header, queue-review, support-export, activity-center, and audit-event surfaces. Raw URLs, raw tokens, raw callback bodies, and raw provider payloads never appear here.

provider_actor_class.description: Frozen provider-actor class vocabulary reused from schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json. A generic 'Connected' state is forbidden on every product, CLI, notification, support-export, activity-center, and audit surface; 'unknown_actor_class' is repair-only and MUST NOT be collapsed with any other class.

provider_class.description: Frozen provider-class vocabulary reused from schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json. Every provider-linked header names exactly one class so notifications, activity-center rows, exports, and audit events can quote the same class without a generic 'External' or 'Cloud' fallback.

trust_posture.description: ADR-0001 workspace-trust state the header was captured under.

redaction_class.description: Declared redaction class reused from ADR-0007 and ADR-0010 redaction posture. Raw URLs, raw tokens, raw callback bodies, raw delegated-token bodies, raw policy-injector material, and raw provider payloads never cross this boundary regardless of class.

object_type_class.description: Provider-side object type the header references. Aligned with object_class members in schemas/providers/effective_scope_resolution.schema.json so a single header can be rendered alongside its scope-resolution row.

object_identity.description: Provider-side identity of the object the header surfaces.

local_or_provider_source_class.description: Frozen vocabulary that names which side currently owns the truth the header is rendering. local_authoritative is a not-yet-published local artifact. provider_authoritative is a remote-owned object the local side is a typed companion to. mirror_derived is provider state served through a self-hosted or organisation mirror that MUST disclose its mirror posture. imported_snapshot_only is a read-only cached shadow whose source is an imported snapshot. local_draft_only_no_provider_state is a draft that has not yet been linked to any provider object.

freshness_tier_class.description: Frozen freshness-tier vocabulary the header renders. live_authoritative is reserved for objects whose authoritative side was reached within the freshness floor. recently_synced_fresh is a cached refresh inside the freshness floor. bounded_stale is older than the freshness floor but inside an admin-bounded staleness window. unbounded_stale is past the bounded window and MUST visibly degrade. unknown_freshness_repair_required is repair-only and MUST be paired with a header_degradation_event_record.

edit_mode_class.description: Frozen edit-mode vocabulary disclosed on the header. draft is a local authoring context whose mutation mode is local_draft. publish is a publish path (publish_now or deferred_publish) running under a typed approval ticket. browser_only is an open_in_provider path that routes through a typed browser_handoff_packet (a generic 'Open in browser' control without a typed packet is forbidden). inspect_only is a read-only view of imported provider state. blocked_for_repair is a repair-only state and MUST be paired with a header_degradation_event_record carrying a repair_hook_ref.

mutation_mode.description: Mutation mode reused from ADR-0010. The header's edit_mode_class projects exactly one mutation mode at a time; surfaces MAY NOT silently widen the mode.

degradation_class.description: Frozen header-degradation vocabulary. Every degradation event names exactly one class so the surface can render a typed downgrade rather than disappearing or silently keeping its claim of authority.

retained_action_class.description: Frozen retained-action vocabulary the header renders when it has degraded. Inspect / copy / export remain available where safe; surfaces MAY NOT remove every action and leave the header empty. Copy actions carry opaque refs only; raw URLs, raw tokens, and raw provider bodies are never exposed through these actions.

removed_action_class.description: Frozen removed-action vocabulary the degradation event explicitly names. Surfaces MUST quote the removed actions so a reviewer can tell the header is degraded rather than missing data; silent removal is forbidden.

reuse_surface_class.description: Frozen reuse-surface vocabulary. Every handoff_reason_reuse_record names exactly one surface so provider class, canonical host, scope, freshness tier, handoff reason, and acting actor class survive together outside the originating panel. A surface that cannot be placed in this list MUST fall through to support_export_summary or audit_event_payload rather than invent a surface-local label.

reason_code.description: Frozen handoff reason-code vocabulary reused from schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json. Notifications, activity-center rows, exports, and audit events MUST quote this code; flattening a typed reason into a plain URL is forbidden.

freshness_block.description: Freshness posture for the provider-side state the header points at. bounded_stale and unbounded_stale MUST cite a staleness_rationale; live_authoritative and recently_synced_fresh MAY omit it.

scope_summary.description: Short reviewable sentence naming the tenant / org / project scope and any narrowing the actor is acting under (project_scoped_grant, install scope, policy-injected scope). Raw provider scope tokens never appear here.

tenant_or_org_scope.description: Tenant or org scope the header is bound under. Empty string only when the provider class is genuinely tenantless.

project_scope_ref.description: Opaque ref to the project_scoped_grant_record narrowing this header. Empty string when no narrowing is active.

scope_summary_block.description: Scope summary the header renders alongside provider class, canonical host, freshness tier, and acting identity.

available_actions_block.description: Inspect / copy / export actions currently rendered on the header. Degradation MUST NOT empty all three arrays at once: the contract is that inspect / copy / export remain available where safe rather than disappearing. Copy and export actions carry opaque refs and structured summaries only; raw URLs, raw tokens, and raw provider bodies never enter these projections.

canonical_host.description: Canonical host the provider is bound to. Self-hosted and mirrored deployments substitute this host without changing the local object vocabulary; mirror_derived sources MUST also disclose mirror posture through local_or_provider_source.

last_sync_at.description: Monotonic timestamp of the most recent authoritative refresh. Empty / absent only when local_or_provider_source is local_draft_only_no_provider_state.

current_mutation_mode.description: ADR-0010 mutation mode the header's edit_mode currently projects. draft → local_draft; publish → publish_now or deferred_publish; browser_only → open_in_provider; inspect_only → inspect_only. blocked_for_repair MAY omit a current_mutation_mode.

actor_subject_ref.description: Opaque ref to the human_account_link / installation_or_app_grant / delegated_credential_binding / project_scoped_grant / policy_injected_service_identity record the header is acting under. Empty string only when actor_class is unknown_actor_class.

acting_identity_badge_ref.description: Opaque ref to the acting_identity_badge_record (in connected_account_record.schema.json) the surface renders alongside the header. A header that cannot cite a badge ref MUST route to actor_class = unknown_actor_class and edit_mode = blocked_for_repair.

approval_ticket_ref.description: Opaque ref to the approval-ticket record admitting the current edit mode. Required when edit_mode is publish or browser_only and the underlying mutation mode is publish_now / open_in_provider; empty string for draft / inspect_only / blocked_for_repair.

browser_handoff_packet_ref.description: Opaque ref to the browser_handoff_packet_record the user will be routed through. Required when edit_mode is browser_only; empty string otherwise.

publish_later_queue_item_ref.description: Opaque ref to the publish_later_queue_item_record the header is parked under. Required when edit_mode is publish and current_mutation_mode is deferred_publish; empty string otherwise.

import_session_ref.description: Opaque ref to the import_session_record the header was refreshed by. Required when local_or_provider_source is provider_authoritative, mirror_derived, or imported_snapshot_only; empty string otherwise.

provider_object_relation_ref.description: Opaque ref to the provider_object_relation_record the header is paired with so drift_state_class and next_safe_action_class survive across surfaces.

header_summary.description: Short reviewable sentence the desktop, CLI, notification, support-export, and activity-center surfaces render. Names provider class, canonical host, object type, freshness tier, scope, edit mode, and acting actor class together; the contract forbids any surface that surfaces only a subset.

observed_at.description: Monotonic timestamp the header was minted at. Header consumers re-validate freshness against this timestamp.

allOf.description: browser_only edit_mode MUST cite a browser_handoff_packet_ref. A generic 'Open in browser' control without a typed packet is forbidden.
publish edit_mode MUST cite either an approval_ticket_ref or a publish_later_queue_item_ref so the publish path is auditable.
browser_only edit_mode MUST project current_mutation_mode = open_in_provider; draft → local_draft; inspect_only → inspect_only.
provider_authoritative, mirror_derived, and imported_snapshot_only sources MUST cite an import_session_ref so freshness, actor scope, and trust posture survive on every surface.
local_draft_only_no_provider_state MUST pair with edit_mode draft and MUST NOT cite an import_session_ref.
Every non-local_draft source class MUST cite a provider_side_object_identity. local_draft_only_no_provider_state MAY omit it because no provider-side object exists yet.
actor_class unknown_actor_class MUST pair with edit_mode blocked_for_repair so the header routes to a repair hook rather than continuing to publish under an unresolved actor.
An unbounded_stale freshness tier MUST NOT pair with publish or browser_only edit modes; the header MUST degrade to inspect_only or blocked_for_repair until refreshed.
unknown_freshness_repair_required freshness tier MUST pair with edit_mode blocked_for_repair.

provider_link_header_record.description: One typed provider-linked object header. Renders provider class, canonical host, object type, local-versus-provider source, last-sync time, freshness tier, scope, edit mode (draft / publish / browser-only / inspect-only / blocked-for-repair), and acting actor class together. Local draft state, imported provider state, and browser-only actions remain distinguishable on every surface; a generic 'Open in browser' control on a protected surface is forbidden when the system can name destination class, reason code, and return path through the typed browser_handoff_packet and browser_handoff_sheet records.

originating_browser_handoff_sheet_ref.description: Opaque ref to the browser_handoff_sheet_record the reuse record points back at when the reused element is a handoff (not a header-only render).

reason_code.description: Frozen handoff reason-code reused from schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json. Required when the reuse record points at a handoff; empty (omitted) for header-only reuses that do not carry a handoff reason.

acting_identity_badge_ref.description: Opaque ref to the acting_identity_badge_record so the actor class is rendered with its frozen badge label outside the originating panel.

object_identity_ref.description: Opaque ref to the provider-side object identity preserved across the reuse surface.

reuse_summary.description: Short reviewable sentence the reuse surface renders. Names provider class, canonical host, scope, freshness tier, handoff reason (where present), and acting actor class together; raw URLs, raw tokens, raw callback bodies, and raw provider bodies are never inlined.

handoff_reason_reuse_record.description: One reuse projection of a provider-linked header (or a handoff sheet) outside its originating panel. Notifications, activity-center rows, support exports, audit events, companion provider headers, CLI status lines, AI context captures, and mutation-journal entries read this record so provider class, canonical host, scope, freshness tier, handoff reason, and acting actor class survive together. Surfaces MAY NOT flatten a typed handoff reason into a plain URL or substitute a generic 'External' label.

originating_invalidation_event_ref.description: Opaque ref to the upstream account_invalidation_event_record, effective_scope_invalidation_event_record, or import-session staleness record this degradation was triggered by. Empty string only when the degradation originates locally (freshness drift detected by the local clock).

retained_actions.description: Frozen retained-action list the header continues to expose. The contract requires inspect / copy / export remain available where safe, so this array MUST contain at least one entry; an empty retained list is forbidden. Surfaces render these actions with their typed labels; a generic 'View' or 'Open' fallback is forbidden.

removed_actions.description: Frozen removed-action list the header explicitly disables. Surfaces MUST quote the removed actions so reviewers can tell the header is degraded rather than missing data; silent removal is forbidden.

repair_hook_ref.description: Opaque ref to the repair hook the surface routes the user to (account reselection, browser recovery, step-up authenticator, admin reconciliation, freshness refresh). Required when degradation_class is actor_revoked, actor_suspended, host_mismatch_detected, tenant_switch_detected, approval_ticket_revoked, policy_blocked, freshness_drift_to_unbounded_stale, or provider_unreachable; empty string only for freshness_drift_to_bounded_stale or mirror_derived_disclosure where no admin repair is required.

allOf.description: Severe degradations MUST cite a repair_hook_ref so the surface routes to a concrete reopen path rather than going silent.

header_degradation_event_record.description: One observable degradation transition for a provider-linked header. Names the typed degradation class, the inspect / copy / export actions retained, the actions explicitly removed, and the repair hook for severe degradations. Silent disappearance is forbidden; the header degrades visibly to inspect_only or blocked_for_repair instead.
